channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Why South Africa's leopards shrank to half their normal size
item.description: Genome analysis of a hidden leopard population in South Africa reveals a remarkable evolutionary story of size reduction.

item.title: Scientists discover ancient brain cells that help block distractions
item.description: A small group of neurons in an ancient brain region acts as a built-in focus filter, helping ignore distractions.

item.title: Early humans brought fire into caves 1.8 million years ago
item.description: A study indicates early humans used fire in South Africa's Wonderwerk Cave as early as 1.79 million years ago. Researchers found burned bone...

item.title: Scientists discover hidden 'footprints of death' that may aid virus spread
item.description: Researchers have found a surprising aspect of cell death. Dying cells leave behind tiny 'footprints of death' that could help viruses spread...
